_doc: Binary schema definition for ChefScript. One file per collection.
_usage: Place in schemas/binary/ — loaded automatically at engine startup.
_note: The engine stores records as packed binary in MongoDB BinData fields. Lookup tables translate integers back to strings at render time. Zero text in the database.
_collection_note: The logical collection name used in page data-source attributes.
_binaryCollection_note: The actual MongoDB collection where binary records are stored.

fields.name: priority
fields._note: Fixed lookup. 1 byte. Values are known at schema time.

fields.name: assignee
fields._note: Dynamic lookup. 1 byte. Values are built from data — new assignees get new IDs automatically. Table persisted to _binary_lookups collection.

fields.name: createdAt
fields._note: Unix timestamp. 8 bytes. Converted to RFC3339 string at decode time.

fields.name: subject
fields._note: Dynamic lookup for subject lines. Each unique subject gets an integer ID. The full text lives only in the in-memory lookup table.

fields.name: description
fields._note: Dynamic lookup for descriptions. Same pattern as subject.

_field_types.timestamp: 8 bytes, stored as Unix epoch, decoded to RFC3339
_field_types.lookup: 1 byte (uint8). Maps to a string via lookup table. Fixed values = defined in schema. Dynamic = built from data at runtime and persisted.

_how_it_works: 1. Engine loads this schema at startup
2. Field offsets and sizes are computed automatically
3. When data is inserted, each field value is encoded to its binary type
4. Lookup fields: string → integer ID via lookup table
5. The entire record becomes a fixed-size byte array stored as MongoDB BinData
6. On retrieval, bytes are decoded back to values using the same schema
7. Lookup IDs are translated back to strings from the in-memory table
8. Dynamic lookup tables are persisted to _binary_lookups collection on save
9. Pages receive normal JSON objects — they never know the data was binary
